sevenDayForecast.headline: The risk of Ex-Tropical Cyclone Koji (12U) redeveloping into a tropical cyclone has decreased.
points: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Koji (12U) is located over northern inland Queensland, and is moving west northwest.
It is forecast to move northwest for the remainder of the week, through the Gulf Country in Queensland.
Latest guidance suggests 12U is more likely to dissipate by the weekend, and the risk of it developing into a tropical cyclone has reduced.
If it hasn't dissipated, it could over Gulf of Carpentaria waters on the weekend and early next week.
There is a Low risk of 12U redeveloping into a tropical cyclone from next Monday evening.
